ops.title: ccp (cc-pool) launch is unconditional-when-installed — no config surface
ops.body:
Decision (user, 2026-07-15): every spawned agent's claude invocation launches through cc-pool when the `ccp` binary resolves on PATH — `claudeCommand`/`resumeCommand` prepend `ccp run` (which selects the emptiest pool account and execs claude, forwarding argv verbatim). Deliberately NO config key or escape hatch: `exec.LookPath("ccp")` is the whole gate, same pattern as backend `Available()`. Plain `claude` is the fallback only when ccp is absent. Rejected alternatives: config-gated default-on/off.

Coupled invariant: `wrapPTYHost` (orchestrate/ptywrap.go) must only substitute the resolved absolute claude path when `command[0] == "claude"` — an unconditional argv[0] swap corrupts `ccp run …` into `<claude-path> run …` (matters on superset, the one backend lacking CanCapture).
